programs.programName: Maryland Durable Medical Equipment Reuse Program (DME)

verified.notes: Program has no age, income, or asset limits per official state site; phone from Prince George's County center listed as main contact to call for help.

programs.notes: Program has no age, income, or asset limits per official state site; phone from Prince George's County center listed as main contact to call for help.

verified.notes: No age, income, or asset limits found in official sources; eligibility tied to Medicaid and nursing facility level of care. Phone from MD Dept of Health official page.

programs.notes: No age, income, or asset limits found in official sources; eligibility tied to Medicaid and nursing facility level of care. Phone from MD Dept of Health official page.

verified.notes: Income eligibility is 'no more than 300% of SSI for one person' per MD Dept of Health site (exact USD value requires current SSI lookup, not specified); no official main phone found, only site-specific contacts listed; 'Mt Maryland PACE' refers to unrelated advocacy event

programs.notes: Income eligibility is 'no more than 300% of SSI for one person' per MD Dept of Health site (exact USD value requires current SSI lookup, not specified); no official main phone found, only site-specific contacts listed; 'Mt Maryland PACE' refers to unrelated advocacy event

verified.notes: Program has no income or asset limits. Minimum age is 18 for caregivers of adults 60+, or caregivers of any age with Alzheimer's disease care recipients. Grandparent caregivers must be 55+. Phone number is from official Maryland Department of Aging flyer. Program remains active as of 2026.

programs.notes: Program has no income or asset limits. Minimum age is 18 for caregivers of adults 60+, or caregivers of any age with Alzheimer's disease care recipients. Grandparent caregivers must be 55+. Phone number is from official Maryland Department of Aging flyer. Program remains active as of 2026.
